source.description: A reviewed six-panel pencil-and-colored-pencil process contact sheet.
source.notes: One consistent three-quarter side-view bottlenose dolphin arcs upward above a small water splash on warm off-white paper. The body, beak, fins, tail, facial landmarks, splash, color, and shadow must all appear before the finishing panel.

process_strategy: The sequence locks one rising body gesture and head-to-tail axis, wraps a single dolphin silhouette around it, adds all fins and tail flukes without redrawing the pose, places facial landmarks and the small splash, then introduces restrained blue-gray pencil before final cleanup only.

final_elements.name: rising body gesture, head mass, beak guide, and tail axis
final_elements.description: A long curved gesture, overlapping head and torso masses, a short beak guide, and a tapering tail axis fix one upward-leaping pose.

final_elements.name: arched dolphin silhouette with rounded forehead, beak, back, belly, and tail stock
final_elements.description: One continuous outer contour wraps the construction into a readable bottlenose dolphin while keeping the same crop and angle.

final_elements.name: dorsal fin, two pectoral flippers, and paired tail flukes
final_elements.description: A curved dorsal fin, one full near flipper, one partly hidden far flipper, and two tail flukes complete the established anatomy.

final_elements.name: eye, mouth line, blowhole, belly division, and small water splash
final_elements.description: A small eye, gentle mouth line, blowhole, pale belly boundary, and a few water arcs give the dolphin expression and context.

final_elements.name: blue-gray body color, pale belly, graphite depth, and watery cast shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained blue-gray pencil and soft graphite model the already drawn dolphin and ground the splash without hiding the handmade construction.

frames.visible_job: Places one rising body gesture, overlapping head and torso masses, a short beak guide, and a tapering tail axis at a fixed scale.
introduces: rising body gesture, head mass, beak guide, and tail axis

frames.visible_job: Wraps the guides with one arched dolphin silhouette, defining the rounded forehead, short beak, back, belly, and tail stock.
introduces: arched dolphin silhouette with rounded forehead, beak, back, belly, and tail stock
requires_prior_elements: rising body gesture, head mass, beak guide, and tail axis

frames.visible_job: Adds the dorsal fin, full near flipper, partly hidden far flipper, and paired tail flukes to the established body.
introduces: dorsal fin, two pectoral flippers, and paired tail flukes
requires_prior_elements: arched dolphin silhouette with rounded forehead, beak, back, belly, and tail stock

frames.visible_job: Adds the eye, mouth line, blowhole, belly division, and a small set of water-splash arcs beneath the established leap.
introduces: eye, mouth line, blowhole, belly division, and small water splash
requires_prior_elements: arched dolphin silhouette with rounded forehead, beak, back, belly, and tail stock
dorsal fin, two pectoral flippers, and paired tail flukes

frames.visible_job: Adds restrained blue-gray pencil, a pale belly, soft graphite depth, and a watery shadow to shapes that are already established.
introduces: blue-gray body color, pale belly, graphite depth, and watery cast shadow
requires_prior_elements: arched dolphin silhouette with rounded forehead, beak, back, belly, and tail stock
dorsal fin, two pectoral flippers, and paired tail flukes
eye, mouth line, blowhole, belly division, and small water splash

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens the established dolphin contours, facial landmarks, fin overlaps, restrained color, splash, and watery shadow.
allowed_changes: small highlight clarification on established forms

rejection_checks: No beak, fin, flipper, tail fluke, eye, mouth, blowhole, belly division, splash, color area, or shadow may first appear in the final panel.
The dolphin must retain one upward arc, three-quarter side view, scale, crop, and head-to-tail proportions across all six panels.
Only the visible tip and attachment of the far flipper may be drawn; do not finish hidden anatomy beneath the body.
Reject extra dolphins, fish, waves, lettering, decorative borders, glossy marine illustration, or photographic anatomy.
The finish must retain visible graphite and colored-pencil texture and remain attainable in about twenty-five minutes.
